commented_code:
types: [published]
 
 jobs:
-  automate-release-scripts:
-    # Only run for the main redis/redis repository (not forks)
-    # Note: Only users with write access can publish releases, providing implicit authorization
+  extract-release-info:
     if: github.repository == 'redis/redis'

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -5,33 +5,28 @@ on:
     types: [published]
 
 jobs:
-  automate-release-scripts:
-    # Only run for the main redis/redis repository (not forks)
-    # Note: Only users with write access can publish releases, providing implicit authorization
+  extract-release-info:
     if: github.repository == 'redis/redis'
discussion_comments.comment_body: this condition is relevant for all jobs. Will the rest of the jobs that don't need extract-release-info won't run if the repo is different? Maybe there's a way to place the if for the entire workflow?

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -5,33 +5,28 @@ on:
     types: [published]
 
 jobs:
-  automate-release-scripts:
-    # Only run for the main redis/redis repository (not forks)
-    # Note: Only users with write access can publish releases, providing implicit authorization
+  extract-release-info:
     if: github.repository == 'redis/redis'
discussion_comments.comment_body:
All other jobs depend either directly on indirectly(via `needs`) on `extract-release-info`, if it gets skipped, so will the all others job that need it.
Exception is the summary job, that is why it has its own verification for the repo.

commented_code:
+name: Post-Release Automation
+
+on:
+  release:
+    types: [published]
+
+jobs:
+  automate-release-scripts:
+    # Only run for the main redis/redis repository (not forks)
+    # Note: Only users with write access can publish releases, providing implicit authorization
+    if: github.repository == 'redis/redis' || github.repository == 'stav-nachmias/fork-redis'

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,156 @@
+name: Post-Release Automation
+
+on:
+  release:
+    types: [published]
+
+jobs:
+  automate-release-scripts:
+    # Only run for the main redis/redis repository (not forks)
+    # Note: Only users with write access can publish releases, providing implicit authorization
+    if: github.repository == 'redis/redis' || github.repository == 'stav-nachmias/fork-redis'

discussion_comments.comment_body: why we need cpp and asan? We don't run this ci with ASAM.

gcc-4.8 → depends on → cpp-4.8
gcc-4.8 → depends on → libgcc-4.8-dev → depends on → libasan0
